name: BytesInFromSource
definition: Bytes entering the NAT gateway from private-side sources; request-side egress baseline.
absence_means: No request-side traffic, missing collector coverage, or wrong namespace prefix.
cardinality_footguns: Prefer nat gateway id over workload-level identity to avoid cardinality explosion.

name: BytesOutToDestination
definition: Bytes forwarded from the NAT gateway to external destinations; confirms outbound forwarding volume.
absence_means: No forwarded outbound traffic, idle gateway, or ingest gap.
cardinality_footguns: Do not group by low-level source IP unless Flow Logs prove bounded normalization.

name: BytesInFromDestination
definition: Bytes returning from external destinations into the NAT gateway; response-path volume proxy.
absence_means: No return traffic, stalled dependencies, or missing data.
cardinality_footguns: Interpret with request-side metrics; response traffic is naturally asymmetric.

name: BytesOutToSource
definition: Bytes forwarded from the NAT gateway back to private-side sources; confirms response delivery.
absence_means: No responses returned to workloads, no traffic, or ingest gap.
cardinality_footguns: Avoid grouping by ephemeral source address if collector exports raw IPs.

name: PacketsInFromSource
definition: Packets entering the NAT gateway from private-side sources; request packet-rate baseline.
absence_means: No request-side packets or missing ingestion.
cardinality_footguns: Packet metrics can be noisier than bytes for chatty protocols.

name: PacketsOutToDestination
definition: Packets forwarded from NAT gateway to external destinations; outbound forwarding confirmation.
absence_means: No forwarded packets, idle gateway, or missing coverage.
cardinality_footguns: Pair with request-side packet metrics to spot forwarding gaps.

name: PacketsInFromDestination
definition: Packets returning from external destinations into the NAT gateway; response-path packet baseline.
absence_means: No return packets, dependency stall, or collector gap.
cardinality_footguns: Do not mistake natural packet asymmetry for packet loss without other signals.

name: PacketsOutToSource
definition: Packets forwarded from NAT gateway back to private-side sources; response delivery proxy.
absence_means: No response packets delivered, no traffic, or ingest gap.
cardinality_footguns: Prefer nat gateway id over subnet id for default groupings.

name: ConnectionAttemptCount
definition: New outbound connection attempts observed by the NAT gateway; baseline for success and failure ratios.
absence_means: No new connection demand or missing metric coverage.
cardinality_footguns: A single hot destination can create pressure even if total attempts remain modest.

name: ConnectionEstablishedCount
definition: Connections successfully established by the NAT gateway; direct proxy for translation success.
absence_means: No successful connections, no demand, or wrong namespace mapping.
cardinality_footguns: Interpret with attempts to distinguish idle from failing.

name: ActiveConnectionCount
definition: Concurrent active translated connections; core concurrency and saturation gauge.
absence_means: Gateway is idle, absent, or data is missing.
cardinality_footguns: Do not normalize or rate this gauge.

name: ErrorPortAllocation
definition: Failed port allocations for new connections; direct sign that the gateway could not find a source port.
absence_means: No port exhaustion observed or no connection demand.
cardinality_footguns: Keep windows tight; averages can hide short failure bursts.

name: IdleTimeoutCount
definition: Connections closed after 350 seconds of inactivity; lifecycle/keepalive risk proxy rather than direct availability signal.
absence_means: No idle expiries, no long-lived idle flows, or missing metric coverage.
cardinality_footguns: Can spike because of protocol behavior, not gateway failure.

name: PacketsDropCount
definition: Packets dropped by the NAT gateway; strong forwarding-failure symptom.
absence_means: No packet drops, no traffic, or collector gap.
cardinality_footguns: Use with packet baselines to avoid overreacting to tiny absolute counts.

name: PeakBytesPerSecond
definition: Peak byte throughput observed during the minute; burst headroom proxy.
absence_means: No burst recorded, idle gateway, or missing data.
cardinality_footguns: Peak and average should be read together; peak alone can overstate steady-state risk.

name: PeakPacketsPerSecond
definition: Peak packet throughput observed during the minute; packet-intensity headroom proxy.
absence_means: No burst recorded, idle gateway, or missing data.
cardinality_footguns: Packet-heavy workloads can stress limits before byte-heavy ones.

name: NOT_APPLICABLE
definition: The base NAT Gateway metric family does not expose direct cost metrics. Use throughput and active-connection efficiency as cost-adjacent proxies, but keep cost out of the core dashboard until a billing data source is available.
absence_means: Not applicable in the metric family.
cardinality_footguns: Do not invent cost widgets from incomplete usage signals.
